name: The Tides Wharf Restaurant
tagline: Fresh seafood and bay views in Bodega Bay
seoDescription: The Tides Wharf & Restaurant serves fresh local seafood with sweeping views of the working harbor in Bodega Bay — breakfast, lunch, and dinner, every day

items.title: Fresh Local Seafood
items.description: Cioppino, whole Dungeness crab, and the signature clam chowder, sourced from the Sonoma Coast.

items.title: Breakfast
items.description: A full breakfast menu daily, with the espresso bar opening early at 7am.

items.title: Lunch & Dinner
items.description: Bay-view dining for lunch and dinner, seven days a week.

items.title: Fish Market
items.description: An on-site market for fresh fish and shellfish to take home.

sections.heading: About The Tides Wharf
body: Set on a working wharf in Bodega Bay, The Tides Wharf & Restaurant pairs fresh local seafood with sweeping views of the harbor, where guests watch fishing boats come and go and sea lions, otters, and shorebirds work the water. The original Tides Wharf is part of local lore as a filming location for Alfred Hitchcock’s The Birds.

Open for breakfast, lunch, and dinner every day of the year, the restaurant is a classic Sonoma Coast destination — known for its to-go clam chowder, whole Dungeness crab, and cioppino, plus an espresso bar and an on-site fish market.
highlights: Fresh local seafood — chowder, crab, cioppino
A Bodega Bay landmark since the Hitchcock era
